title: Parallelize LLM spawns only; serialize file writes via sync JS I/O guarantee
context: backfill's latency is sum of per-commit Claude synthesis times. Parallelizing file I/O too would race writes. Parallelizing only expensive LLM spawns is safe because JS execution is single-threaded.
decision: Introduced mapPool() bounded worker pool to run up to N LLM spawns concurrently (--concurrency flag, default 4). File writes remain atomic and sequential (JS code runs between await boundaries). reindex() executes once after pool drains.
consequences: ~4x speedup on LLM-bound repos (linear scaling with concurrency)
Trivial commits unaffected (free heuristic path unchanged)
New code: mapPool() helper + --concurrency CLI flag
Safety depends on JS single-threaded execution guarantee between await boundaries
alternatives_rejected: Full parallelization of commits: decision file writes would race and corrupt
Sequential processing (status quo): unacceptable latency on deep repositories
Promise.all with pre-spawned workers: no backpressure, uncontrolled concurrency
